note: CORRECTED 2026-06-16: L106 fix. Original scorecard (v1) used no_trade_before=10:00 default instead of production 09:35, and entry_no_trade_window_et:null was silently dropped keeping legacy 14:00-15:00 window active. Both bugs fixed in _params_to_kwargs. Production-correct run (09:35 gate, no_trade_window=None) gives different baseline (n=97 IS vs v1 n=54). V1 WF=5.794 was an artifact.

oos_window_v2.notes: 3 of 7 removed trades were profitable. Filter over-aggressive in May recovery regime.

walk_forward_v2.interpretation: IS improvement of ,267 generalizes to only 63 OOS. Filter helps catastrophe months (April tariff-shock: +,584) but hurts recovery months (May: -,421). Regime-conditional, not universal.

j_edge_score.5_04_status: CAPTURED — 5/04 +04 preserved in dur=8 (anchor +04 both baseline and candidate)
j_edge_score.note: 4/29 and 5/01 early-morning anchors now INCLUDED in baseline (09:35 gate fixes this). 4/29 in OOS baseline.

sw1.label: April 2026-04-01..2026-04-30
sw1.result: PASS — dur=8 beats baseline by +,584 (tariff-shock filtered)

sw2.label: May 2026-05-01..2026-05-22
sw2.result: FAIL — dur=8 HURTS baseline by -,421 (filter over-aggressive in recovery regime)

sub_window_stability_v2.verdict: FAIL — April passes but May fails. Filter is regime-conditional: works in catastrophe months, hurts in trending recovery months.

ratification_gates_v2.sub_window_stable: FAIL — May sub-window shows filter hurts (-,421)
ratification_gates_v2.auto_ratify_blocker: WF=0.072 < 0.7 threshold; May sub-window FAIL; oos_n=14 < 20

root_cause_v1_failure.details: Old scorecard used no_trade_before=10:00 (function default) instead of production 09:35. Also entry_no_trade_window_et:null was silently ignored, keeping legacy (14:00,15:00) no-trade window. Both bugs fixed 2026-06-16.
root_cause_v1_failure.impact: V1 baseline missed 43 IS and 8 OOS early-morning trades. V1 OOS baseline was -,907 (all 09:35-10:00 OOS trades were profitable; excluding them made baseline look terrible). V1 WF=5.794 was entirely an artifact of the wrong baseline.

next_steps: Research: is dur=8 filter useful with VIX-regime conditioning (escalating VIX only)?
Research: would a larger threshold (dur=12 or dur=15) avoid removing May recovery trades?
Research: compare May morning vs afternoon dur distributions to understand which trades are removed
overall_verdict: FAILED — WF=0.072 (threshold 0.7). Production-correct params reveal filter is regime-conditional: helps April catastrophe (+,584) but hurts May recovery (-,421). Not safe to ratify unconditionally. See next_steps for potential VIX-gated variant.
